questions.text: The order of a differential equation is defined as:
options: The highest power of the independent variable
The order of the highest order derivative present
The sum of all powers of derivatives
questions.explanation: The order is the highest derivative that appearing in the equation.

questions.text: The degree of a differential equation is the power of the highest order derivative, provided the equation is a ________ in its derivatives.
questions.explanation: A differential equation must be expressible as a polynomial in its derivatives to have a defined degree.

questions.text: What is the order and degree of (dВІy/dxВІ)Ві + (dy/dx)ВІ + sin(dy/dx) + 1 = 0?
questions.explanation: The highest derivative is 2nd order. The degree is not defined because the equation is not a polynomial in derivatives due to the sin(dy/dx) term.

questions.text: A general solution of a differential equation contains ________ arbitrary constants.
options: Equal to the order of the equation
questions.explanation: The number of arbitrary constants in a general solution matches the order of the differential equation.

questions.text: A particular solution of a differential equation contains ________ arbitrary constants.
questions.explanation: A particular solution is obtained by assigning specific values to the arbitrary constants of the general solution.

questions.text: Which method is used to solve dy/dx = f(x)g(y)?
questions.explanation: We can write dy/g(y) = f(x) dx and integrate both sides.

questions.text: A differential equation dy/dx = F(x, y) is homogeneous if F(О»x, О»y) is equal to:
questions.explanation: A function is homogeneous of degree zero if scaling both variables by О» does not change the function value.

questions.text: To solve a homogeneous differential equation, the standard substitution is:
questions.explanation: Substituting y = vx reduces the homogeneous equation to a variable separable form.

questions.text: For a linear differential equation dy/dx + Py = Q (where P, Q are functions of x), the Integrating Factor (I.F.) is:
questions.explanation: Multiplying the equation by e^(вҲ« P dx) makes the left side a perfect derivative of (y * I.F.).

questions.text: The solution of the linear differential equation dy/dx + Py = Q is given by:
options: y * I.F. = вҲ« (Q * I.F.) dx + C
questions.explanation: Standard solution format for first-order linear differential equations.

questions.text: Find the order of the differential equation: dВіy/dxВі + 2(dВІy/dxВІ)ВІ - dy/dx + y = 0.
questions.explanation: The highest order derivative present is the 3rd derivative.

questions.text: Which of the following is a homogeneous differential equation?
questions.explanation: In (x - y) dy = (x + 2y) dx, dy/dx = (x + 2y)/(x - y). Both numerator and denominator are linear polynomials.

questions.text: The integrating factor of dy/dx - y/x = 2xВІ is:
questions.explanation: P = -1/x. I.F. = e^(вҲ« -1/x dx) = e^(-log x) = e^(log 1/x) = 1/x.

questions.text: The number of arbitrary constants in the particular solution of a differential equation of order 3 is:
questions.explanation: Particular solutions have no arbitrary constants as they represent a specific member of the solution family.

questions.text: Solving dy/dx = e^(x-y) leads to the solution:
questions.explanation: dy/dx = e^x / e^y => e^y dy = e^x dx. Integrating gives e^y = e^x + C.

questions.text: A linear differential equation of the form dx/dy + PвӮҒx = QвӮҒ has integrating factor:
questions.explanation: When x is the dependent variable, we integrate PвӮҒ with respect to y.

questions.text: Order of the differential equation of family of circles passing through origin and having centers on x-axis is:
questions.explanation: Equation is (x-a)ВІ + yВІ = aВІ, which simplifies to xВІ + yВІ - 2ax = 0. Since there is only one arbitrary constant 'a', the order is 1.

questions.text: The differential equation y' + y = 1 is:
questions.explanation: It is linear (dy/dx + Py = Q) and also variable separable (dy / (1-y) = dx).

questions.text: Degree of differential equation (y'')ВІ + (y')Ві + yвҒҙ = 0 is:
questions.explanation: The highest order derivative is y'' (2nd order), and its power is 2.

questions.text: The general solution of dy/dx = y/x is:
questions.explanation: dy/y = dx/x => log y = log x + log k => y = kx.

questions.text: Integrating factor of cos x dy/dx + y sin x = 1 is:
questions.explanation: Divide by cos x: dy/dx + y tan x = sec x. P = tan x. I.F. = e^(вҲ« tan x dx) = e^(log sec x) = sec x.

questions.text: The differential equation representing family of curves y = e^(cx) is:
questions.explanation: log y = cx => c = (log y) / x. Differentiating y = e^(cx) gives y' = c e^(cx) = cy. Substituting c gives y' = y (log y)/x.

questions.text: The order of differential equation of all parabolas with axis parallel to y-axis is:
questions.explanation: Equation is y = axВІ + bx + c. Three arbitrary constants mean the order is 3.

questions.text: Integration Factor of (1 - xВІ) dy/dx - xy = 1 is:
questions.explanation: Standard linear form solution: P = -x/(1-xВІ). вҲ« P dx = 1/2 log(1-xВІ). I.F. = e^(log вҲҡ(1-xВІ)).

questions.text: General solution of dy/dx = 1 + x + y + xy is:
questions.explanation: dy/dx = (1+x)(1+y) => dy/(1+y) = (1+x) dx. Integrating gives the result.

questions.text: A differential equation is called linear if the dependent variable and its derivatives appear in:
questions.explanation: Also, they should not be multiplied together.

questions.text: Solution of dy/dx = cos x is:
questions.explanation: Direct integration of the function of x.

questions.text: Formation of differential equation involves ________ arbitrary constants.
questions.explanation: To find the DE of a family of curves, we differentiate and remove the constants.

questions.text: Integrating factor of x dy/dx - y = 2xВІ is:
questions.explanation: dy/dx - y/x = 2x. P = -1/x. I.F. = 1/x.

questions.text: The degree of differential equation dВІy/dxВІ + (dy/dx)Ві + yвҒҙ = 0 is:
questions.explanation: The power of the highest order derivative (dВІy/dxВІ) is 1.

questions.text: The particular solution of dy/dx = 2x passing through (0, 1) is:
questions.explanation: Integrating dy = 2x dx gives y = xВІ + C. At (0,1), 1 = 0 + C, so C=1.

questions.text: The general solution of dy/dx + y = 0 is:
questions.explanation: dy/y = -dx => log y = -x + k => y = eвҒ»ЛЈвҒәбөҸ = CeвҒ»ЛЈ.

questions.text: The number of arbitrary constants in the general solution of a DE of order 'n' is:
questions.explanation: A general solution always contains as many arbitrary constants as the order of the differential equation.

questions.text: The integrating factor of dy/dx + P(x)y = Q(x) is intended to make the left side:
questions.explanation: Multiplying by I.F. allows the LHS to be written as d/dx(y * I.F.).

questions.text: Degree of the differential equation (dВІy/dxВІ)ВІ + sin(y) = 0 is:
questions.explanation: The equation is a polynomial in derivatives. sin(y) does not affect the degree; only sin(dy/dx) would.

questions.text: The order of the differential equation of all non-vertical lines in a plane is:
questions.explanation: The equation is y = mx + c. There are two arbitrary constants (m and c), so the order is 2.

questions.text: Which of the following substitutions is used for dx/dy = h(x/y)?
questions.explanation: For equations where x/y is the functional form, we substitute x = vy.

questions.text: The solution of dy/dx = eвҒ»Кё is:
questions.explanation: eКё dy = dx => Integrating both sides gives eКё = x + C.

questions.text: Integrating factor for dy/dx - y = 1 is:
questions.explanation: P = -1, so I.F. = e^(вҲ«-1 dx) = eвҒ»ЛЈ.

questions.text: The differential equation of the family of curves y = a cos(x + b) is of order:
questions.explanation: There are two arbitrary constants 'a' and 'b', hence the order is 2.
